content.problemStatement:
Each lemonade costs $5. Customers arrive one by one and pay using a $5, $10, or $20 bill.
Initially, the shop has no money for change.

For each customer:


If they pay with $5 → no change is needed.
If they pay with $10 → you must give back $5.
If they pay with $20 → you must give back $15 using available $5 and/or $10 bills.
The function must determine whether it is possible to serve all customers in order while always giving correct change.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecanProvideChangecanProvideChangecanProvideChange – This function checks whether it is possible to give correct change to every customer at a lemonade stand.

Parameters
customerBillscustomerBillscustomerBills : A reference to an array of integers where each element represents the bill paid by a customer.

Each value is one of {5, 10, 20}.
Each lemonade costs exactly $5.


numberOfBillsnumberOfBillsnumberOfBills : The number of elements in the customerBills array.
Return Value
Returns truetruetrue if correct change can be provided to every customer.
Returns falsefalsefalse otherwise.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤n≤1051 \leq n \leq 10^51≤n≤105
customerBills[i]∈{5,10,20}customerBills[i] \in \{5, 10, 20\}customerBills[i]∈{5,10,20}
Sum of nnn over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains a single integer nnn — the number of customers.
The second line contains nnn space-separated integers representing the array customerBillscustomerBillscustomerBills.




Output Format

For each test case, print a boolean value:

truetruetrue if correct change can be provided to all customers.
falsefalsefalse otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For first test case:

Customer 1: pays 5 -> no change needed (5’s = 1)
Customer 2: pays 5 -> no change needed (5’s = 2)
Customer 3: pays 10 -> give back 5 (5’s = 1, 10’s = 1)
Customer 4: pays 5 -> no change needed (5’s = 2)
Customer 5: pays 20 -> must give back 10 + 5 ? possible (5’s = 1, 10’s = 0)
Customer 6: pays 20 -> need 15 change -> impossible (no 10 left) 
Result: false



For third test case:

First four customers pay 5 -> store 4 fives
Customer 5: pays 10 -> give back 5 (5’s = 3, 10’s = 1)
Customer 6: pays 10 -> give back 5 (5’s = 2, 10’s = 2)
Customer 7: pays 20 -> give back 10 + 5 (5’s = 1, 10’s = 1)
Customer 8: pays 20 ? give back 10 + 5 (5’s = 0, 10’s = 0) 
All customers fulfilled.



Result: true
